facts.text: The city was named after William Bingham, a wealthy Philadelphian who bought the 10,000 acre patent for the land in 1786.
Surrounded by rolling hills, it lies in the Southern Tier region near the Pennsylvania border, in a bowl-shaped valley at the confluence of the Susquehanna and Chenango rivers.
From the days of the railroad, Binghamton became a transportation crossroads, with the Erie Railroad arriving in 1849 and the Delaware, Lackawanna and Western Railroad soon after.
The Eastern League, one of the older Double‑A baseball leagues, was founded at the Arlington Hotel in downtown Binghamton in 1923.
During World War II, growth continued as IBM, founded in greater Binghamton, emerged as a global technology leader.
